questions.question: June 2021? $24000D$23000C$15000B$14000A What is shown in a partnership appropriation account?22 the capital contributed by each partnerA the distribution of profit/loss among the partnersB the partners’ current account balancesC the interest on a partner’s loan account D Rajid and Sunil formed a partnership on 1 January 2019 but did not prepare a partnership agreement. They provided the following information. 23 SunilRajid $20000$40000capital introduced 1 January 2019 during the year ended 31 December 2019 $3500$5000drawings 50%50%share of work They decided to draw up a partnership agreement for future years. Which item would be most beneficial to Rajid in 2020? interest on capitalA interest on drawingsB limit on annual drawingsC partnership salariesD 06_7707_11_2024_1.1 8 A company supplied the following information.24 $ 300000 45000 8 000 35000 25000 issued and paid up share capital general reserve long-term bank loan retained earnings 6% debentures What is the total equity? $413000D$380000C$345000B$335000A Which apply to both debentures and preference shares? 25 included in capital employed fixed rate of return no voting rights ✓✓✓ A ✓✗✓ B ✗✓✗ C ✗✗✗ D 06_7707_11_2024_1.1 9 The AB Sports Club runs a café for the use of members. The treasurerprepared draft financial statements which showed a deficit of $700 and an accumulated fund of $4600. He then discovered that the closing inventory of the café had been overvalued by $50. How much were the deficit and the accumulated fundafterthe inventory valuation had been corrected? 26 accumulated fund $ deficit $ 4550650A 4650650B 4550750C 4650750D Which accountingstatement prepared by the treasurer of a sports club provides a summary of all the monies received and paid out during the financial year? 27 bank reconciliation statementA income and expenditure accountB receipts and payments accountC statement of financial positionD At the end of the financial year, a manufacturing business owed rent on its factory. The business adjusted its financial statements to account for this. What effects did this adjustment have? 28 gross profitcost of productionprime cost increasedecreasedecreaseA decreaseincreaseno effectB decreaseno effectno effectC decreaseincreaseincreaseD 06_7707_11_2024_1.1 10 What is a direct cost for a manufacturer?29 carriage outwardsA depreciationof delivery vehicleB factory supervisor’s salaryC production royaltiesD, A business uses 20% mark up to arrive at its selling prices. During the year it made purchases of $35000 and the inventory decreased from $7000 to $2000. What was the revenue for the year? 30 $48000D$42000C$40000B$36000A What is needed for the preparation of a statement of affairs?31 a trial balanceA an income statementB books of prime entryC values of assets and liabilitiesD, A trader provided the following information.32 $ 146000cash purchases for the year 108000credit purchases for the year 14000trade payables at the year end What was the trader’s trade payables turnover? 6 daysA 13 daysB 21 daysC

A business uses 20% mark up to arrive at its selling prices. During the year it made purchases of $35000 and the inventory decreased from $7000 to $2000. What was the revenue for the year? 30 $48000D$42000C$40000B$36000A What is needed for the preparation of a statement of affairs?31 a trial balanceA an income statementB books of prime entryC values of assets and liabilitiesD

questions.question: daysD 06_7707_11_2024_1.1 11 George provided the following information.33 $ 15000 12000 18000 8 000 non-current assets inventory trade receivables trade payables His liquid (acid test) ratio was 1.2:1. What was his bank overdraft? $29500D$17000C$15000B$7000A The following ratios have been calculated for a trader.34 year 2year 1 20% 6% 15% 9% profit margin return on capital employed (ROCE) What explains these changes? Drawings have increased by more than profit for the year.A Gross profit has increased but profit for the year has decreased.B Profit for the year has increased and capital has been introduced.C Profit for the year has increased and a long-term loan has been repaid.D William is a trader. He has received an order from James to supply goods on credit. When should William regard the revenue as earned? 35 when William accepts the order from JamesA when William delivers the goods to JamesB when William receives full payment for the goods from JamesC when William receives the first payment on account from JamesD 2024 12 Permission to reproduce items where third-party owned material protected by copyright is included has been sought and cleared where possible.
